_comment: Copy this to roboframe.config.json and fill in your values. The file is gitignored. Per-service .env files keep working — env vars override anything in this file.
_accessTokenComment: Read-mostly token shared by every WebSocket consumer (kiosks, node-display, spatialstash). Required to connect to /rpc/ws but cannot call privileged `rpcsend`. Top-level so the same value can be copied to client and server configs.
_deviceIdComment: Identifier this kiosk joins channels and HA entities under. Shared by node-display and native-kiosk on the same host (and by any browser kiosk URL that names this id via ?ws=). Override with the DEVICE_ID env var.

server._comment: The merged Node server: image API + WebSocket broker + optional Home Assistant bridge, all in one process on one port.

ha._comment: Read-only sensor forwarder. With url+token set, the broker subscribes to the listed HA entities and pushes their state to kiosks via the `update` action so the frontend can render outdoor temperature, humidity, etc. Set enabled:false to force-disable.

mqtt._comment: MQTT auto-discovery. With url set, the broker connects to the broker (typically the HA Mosquitto add-on), publishes retained discovery for each kiosk's light/binary_sensor/sensor entities, and bridges command-topic writes from HA into setBrightness/displayState messages back to the kiosk. Disabled when url is empty.

display._comment: On-device daemon (per kiosk). Connects to the server's WebSocket and drives backlight / brightness from the messages it pushes.
display._dimToOffSecondsComment: Pi only. Two-stage power-down. A `display off` first dims the panel via DDC (instant, HDMI stays linked, wake is a single setvcp); after this many seconds of continued inactivity it escalates to `xset dpms force off`. Set to 0 to skip the dim stage and go straight to DPMS off. Set to null to never escalate — the display stays in the dim stage indefinitely.

nightLight._comment: Optional. While the local clock falls inside [start, end), PIR-driven wake applies this brightness (user scale 0–255, mapped to DDC % on Pi). Outside the window, PIR wake leaves brightness alone. Set brightness:null to keep the display fully off during the window — PIR motion will not wake it. Set enabled:false to disable.

webcam._comment: Optional. When enabled, node-display captures MJPG from the named V4L2 device and serves it at http://<host>:<port>/stream.mjpg (multipart) and /snapshot.jpg. Toggleable from HA via the auto-discovered switch entity (roboframe/switch/<deviceId>/webcam/set). Linux-only; the camera process only runs while a client is attached.
webcam._comment_controls: Optional. V4L2 controls applied via `v4l2-ctl --set-ctrl` each time capture (re)starts. Applied in listed order, so put dependent controls last (e.g. auto_exposure before exposure_time_absolute). Common UVC fixes: exposure_dynamic_framerate=0 stops the camera dropping fps to lengthen exposure in dim light; power_line_frequency=1 is 50 Hz (EU) / 2 is 60 Hz (US) anti-flicker. Run `v4l2-ctl -d <device> --list-ctrls` to see what your camera supports.
webcam._comment_tokens: Optional. When non-empty, every media endpoint (/stream.mjpg, /snapshot.jpg, /audio.pcm) requires one of these tokens via `?token=<t>` or `Authorization: Bearer <t>`. /health stays open. Use a long random value, e.g. `openssl rand -hex 24`. Gates both the LAN port and any tailscale-serve path.
webcam._comment_audio: Optional. When enabled, captures raw PCM from an ALSA device via `arecord` and serves it at /audio.pcm (little-endian, no container). Pair it with the video URL in a consumer that muxes separate A/V inputs (e.g. Scrypted FFmpeg camera: `-f s16le -ar <rate> -ac <channels> -i http://<host>:<port>/audio.pcm`). The mic process only runs while a client is attached.

kiosk._comment: Optional. Native SDL2 frontend (native-kiosk/kiosk.py) — a Chromium replacement for memory-constrained boards (Pi 3) running X11 + Openbox. Connects to the same WebSocket as the browser kiosk and renders pixels with pygame + Pillow. Env overrides: WS_URL, ACCESS_TOKEN, INTERVAL, BRIGHT, LOWMEM, MOD_TAGS.
